steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: Text 2 concedes that studies report positive results but then pivots: methodological shortcomings — illustrated by Hansemark's 19-student sample — make it impossible to draw firm conclusions. The correct answer must capture this critique of study design, not the content of the results themselves.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — [overstates the evidence]: Text 2 does not claim the programs definitively work — it disputes whether existing studies can establish that. ✗
  • Choice B — 'results are unknown because studies lack adequate design for definitive conclusions': This directly reflects Text 2's argument that small, flawed samples prevent any firm verdict on program effectiveness. ✓
  • Choice C — [misses the target of the critique]: Text 2's objection is about methodology, not about the programs' goals or content. ✗
  • Choice D — [introduces claims Text 2 does not make]: Text 2 does not argue the programs harm participants or produce negative outcomes. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
